channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Five killed in shooting at youth welfare centre in Germany’s Stade
item.description: Police say several others were injured and the suspected attacker has been arrested.

item.title: Sudan says China has waived $50m loan: What’s in it for Khartoum, Beijing?
item.description: China and Sudan signed off on a waiver of $50m as Sudan’s military-led government seeks support amid Western sanctions.

item.title: Venezuela earthquake rescues: Five heart-warming moments
item.description: Amid the death and destruction, a few fleeting moments have brought rare relief to Venezuelans.

item.title: Israeli attack in Gaza kills three, including a child
item.description: The strike in Deir el-Balah is the latest Israeli attack amid ongoing 'ceasefire' violations.

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s early FIFA World Cup exit prompts federation chief to resign
item.description: Saudi Arabia, the three-time Asian champions, finish bottom of Group H with two points from three games.

item.title: Netherlands vs Morocco: FIFA World Cup Round of 32: Prediction, team news
item.description: A rare meeting of the top 10 ranked teams sees Morocco take on the Netherlands in the round of 32 in Monterrey, Mexico.

item.title: ‘A concession to Zelenskyy’s ultimatum’: Ukraine’s triumph over Belarus
item.description: President Zelenskyy demands Lukashenko shut down stations installed by Russia 'or we will'.

item.title: Nigerians return home after fleeing xenophobic attacks in South Africa
item.description: Nigerians are returning home after facing a wave of xenophobic violence in South Africa.

item.title: Vance Vs Rubio: Is Team Trump divided on Iran and Lebanon?
item.description: The White House denied a split between the two officials who have historically held diverging foreign policy positions.

item.title: ‘Tit-for-tat US-Iran attacks appear to be over’
item.description: Former US Ambassador to Oman Richard Schmierer says recent US-Iran military exchanges appear to have run their course.

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war: Why has Putin rejected limits on long-range strikes?
item.description: Russian president claims Ukraine, which has ramped up attacks on Russia's energy sector, proposed the limits.

item.title: ‘Erased from history’: A century on from Canada’s anti-Greek riots
item.description: Historians and Greek community members say 1918 riots in Toronto reminiscent of anti-immigrant rhetoric, xenophobia now.

item.title: Australia and Vanuatu sign deal to block foreign military bases
item.description: Two countries sign economic and security pact, as China expresses concern that the agreement may be targeted at it.

item.title: China slaps export controls on dozens of Japanese entities
item.description: China's Ministry of Commerce says measures are a response to Tokyo's 'new militarism'.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Round of 32 schedule, predictions and latest news
item.description: World Cup round of 32 continues with Brazil, Germany and the Netherlands in action as Canada celebrates history.

item.title: Venezuela rescuers race against the clock to find more survivors
item.description: Venezuela rescuers race against the clock to find more survivors.

item.title: Will Andy Burnham mark a shift in the UK’s Gaza policy?
item.description: The Labour Party has the chance to review its policy on Israel, but observers say it will opt for continuity.

item.title: UK’s likely next leader Andy Burnham to unveil economic, devolution plans
item.description: Frontrunner to be next PM expected to announce a 10-year mission to raise living standards.

item.title: Israel triggers huge explosion in southern Lebanon
item.description: A massive explosion triggered by the Israeli military shook the southern Lebanese village of Majdal Zoun.

item.title: Watch World Cup Day 18: Canada beats South Africa, through to last 16
item.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

item.title: South Korea announces more than $1 trillion AI, chip investment drive
item.description: South Korean president frames the push as a race against time to secure the country's domination in AI boom.

item.title: Stealing from the gods: India’s Ram Temple hit by corruption scandal
item.description: The temple is mired in embezzlement allegations before crucial state elections, embarrassing PM Modi's government.

item.title: South Korean president, ex-players, fans demand change after World Cup exit
item.description: South Korea's World Cup exit after the group stage prompted the coach to quit, with the president calling for a probe.

item.title: Brazil vs Japan: FIFA World Cup Round of 16 – Vinicius, prediction, lineups
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup round of 32 pits South American giants Brazil against Asian heavyweights Japan in Houston.

item.title: Skydiving plane crash kills 11 in northeastern France
item.description: 11 people were killed when a plane belonging to a parachuting school crashed in Tomblaine, France.
